consumed_sources.authority_use: Supports public methodology boundary planning and agent standard alignment only.
consumed_sources.limitations: Does not authorize public methodology content, CMS packages, publishing, search exposure, runtime mutation, or private payload reuse.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Supports standard-aligned language about the Big Five result-page agent boundary.
consumed_sources.limitations: Report evidence remains handoff/planning evidence and not public page body authority.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Confirms read-only public-boundary clearance for downstream planning.
consumed_sources.limitations: Does not permit raw private attempt access, private result payload access, runtime action, or CMS/search action.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Supports read-only runtime QA integration planning and sanitized blocker interpretation.
consumed_sources.limitations: Ready for QA consumption does not mean ready for runtime, production, CMS, or public methodology content.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Supports analytics quality-report vocabulary for downstream planning.
consumed_sources.limitations: Does not authorize event emission, backfill, opportunity scoring, provider calls, or instrumentation.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Supports claim/privacy/safety gate planning and private-field exclusion.
consumed_sources.limitations: Does not authorize pilot, runtime, production, CMS, search, or opportunity scoring.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Referenced as historical READY_READONLY_CLEARED lineage for Big Five result-page agent planning.
consumed_sources.limitations: PR lineage is not a methodology source ledger and must not be treated as public content authority.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Supports cleared share-safety blocker only at sanitized read-only evidence layer.
consumed_sources.limitations: Does not expose private result payloads and does not authorize fap-api mutation from this fap-web PR.

consumed_sources.limitations: Does not expose private result payloads and does not authorize fap-api mutation from this fap-web PR.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Defines backend-only Big Five result-page V2 asset-agent operating contract.
consumed_sources.limitations: Dedicated to private result-page V2 asset governance; it is not public methodology page authority.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Defines staging-only gates and production boundary for result-page V2 asset-agent work.
consumed_sources.limitations: Does not open runtime, CMS, pilot, or production access.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Defines result-page V2 artifact protocol and public payload exclusions.
consumed_sources.limitations: Docs-only protocol; does not generate assets or public methodology copy.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Maps result-page V2 source authority and staging-only asset governance.
consumed_sources.limitations: Result-page source authority is not automatically methodology/trust/science public-page source authority.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Connects fap-web Big Five result-page shell to backend runbook, gates, schema, and source authority references.
consumed_sources.limitations: Registry linkage proves agent stack ownership, not public methodology content authority.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Shows public ContentPage routes consume CMS-backed ContentPage data through route wrappers.
consumed_sources.limitations: Route rendering is consumer behavior and not editorial authority.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Shows fap-web renders backend/CMS ContentPage payloads.
consumed_sources.limitations: Template copy or fallback behavior must not become source authority.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Defines discoverable ContentPage slugs and CMS/public API adapter boundary.
consumed_sources.limitations: Unreviewed CMS text and stale fallback data remain non-authority for new methodology claims.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Supports that the six research/method ContentPage routes require CMS/backend authority and bounded claims.
consumed_sources.limitations: Does not authorize this PR to edit ContentPage rendering, schema, sitemap, llms, canonical, noindex, or publication state.

consumed_sources.authority_use: Identifies public Big Five profile route behavior as separate from private result-page agent authority.
consumed_sources.limitations: Public profile route must not be generated from private result payloads.

boundary_assertions.assertion: Big Five result-page agent evidence can support public methodology/trust/science boundaries only.
Big Five result-page private payload cannot become public methodology content.
Backend/CMS ContentPage authority is stronger than frontend fallback.
Frontend fallback copy is not authority.
Unreviewed CMS text is not authority.
Result-page source ledger is not automatically methodology source ledger.
Big Five public profile route is separate authority from private result-page agent.
Historical share-safety blocker is cleared only if current origin/main evidence confirms PR #2326/#2331 sanitized evidence.
If evidence conflict remains, record sidecar and preserve HOLD for any affected downstream claim.
FAQPage schema is not enabled unless visible CMS FAQ gate passes.
Sitemap, llms, schema, hreflang, canonical, and indexability changes remain HOLD.
CMS, import, publish, and search actions remain HOLD.

source_gaps.description: A result-page V2 source authority map exists in fap-api read-only evidence, but it is not automatically a public methodology/trust/science source ledger.
Public ContentPage copy requires CMS/backend review and claim gate approval before publication or schema/search exposure.
